decisions.title: Why a Simple While Loop Is the Core Kernel
decisions.description: The entire agent architecture begins with a while loop that reads user input, sends it to an LLM, and prints the response. This is intentional: the loop is the minimal viable agent. Every future capability — tools, memory, subagents — is an addition to this loop, not a replacement. Starting here makes it obvious that an agent is not magic; it is a program that calls an LLM repeatedly.
decisions.alternatives: We considered starting with a framework like LangChain or AutoGen, but that would hide the core mechanism behind abstractions. We also considered an event-driven architecture, but a synchronous loop is easier to reason about when learning.

zh.title: 为什么用简单的 while 循环作为核心内核
zh.description: 整个 Agent 架构从一个 while 循环开始：读取用户输入、发送给 LLM、打印回复。这是刻意为之的——循环就是最小可用的 Agent。后续所有能力（工具、记忆、子 Agent）都是在这个循环上叠加，而非替换。从这里起步，能让你清楚地看到：Agent 不是什么黑魔法，它就是一个反复调用 LLM 的程序。

decisions.title: Why No Tools Are Needed to Start
decisions.description: Lesson 0 deliberately has zero tool definitions. The point is to isolate the conversational loop from tool execution so learners understand that chat completion alone is already useful. Adding tools in the very first lesson would conflate two independent concepts: conversation management and function calling.
decisions.alternatives: Some tutorials introduce tools immediately to show 'the cool stuff' first. We rejected this because it creates a false impression that agents require tools. A pure chat loop is already an agent — just a very limited one.

zh.title: 为什么一开始不引入工具
zh.description: 第 0 课刻意没有定义任何工具。目的是将对话循环与工具执行分离开来，让学习者理解：仅凭聊天补全本身就已经是有用的。如果在第一课就引入工具，会把两个独立的概念——对话管理和函数调用——混为一谈。
